differential_diagnoses: Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated enteropathy
next_steps: Urgent surgical consultation for exploratory laparotomy and resection of the diseased jejunal segments
Perform full-thickness histopathological examination of the resected bowel wall and mesenteric lymph nodes
Conduct immunohistochemical staining (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD56, CD30, TCR-beta, TCR-delta, EBER) on surgical specimens
Perform T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on the suspicious jejunal lesions and the expanded peripheral NK-cell population
Repeat imaging (e.g., PET/CT) to evaluate for systemic lymphoma involvement
Bone marrow biopsy and aspiration if systemic lymphoma is suspected
Discontinue infliximab until the etiology of the ischemic/neoplastic bowel lesions is definitively determined

differential_diagnoses: Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with indolent T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder
Crohn's disease with secondary stricturing and ischemia
next_steps: Urgent exploratory laparotomy with segmental jejunal resection for diagnosis and management of suspected ischemic segments
Full-thickness histopathological examination of resected bowel and mesenteric lymph nodes with flow cytometry and T-cell receptor (TCR) clonality studies
Immunohistochemical panel on surgical specimen (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD56, CD30, TCR-beta, TCR-delta, EBER)
Discontinuation of infliximab given the risk of occult malignancy and persistent bowel ischemia
Whole-body PET/CT staging to assess for systemic nodal or extranodal disease
Repeat peripheral blood flow cytometry to confirm and characterize the clonal NK-cell population
Comprehensive genetic testing for primary immunodeficiency syndromes (e.g., STAT3, CTLA4, LRBA mutations)
